title: A printer for 3d printing
abstract: The printer for 3D printing according to the invention is characterised in that its storage container unit (4) comprises a rectangular frame (43) with peripheral offsets (44), the interior of which is formed by single-mode optical fibres (48) arranged symmetrically to each other, each fibre comprising a light-transporting core (48') and a coating impenetrable to light (48"), whereas upper ends of the optical fibres are permanently joined with lenses (49) under which a stabilising mesh (55) is situated, while lower ends of the optical fibres (48) are straight-through fixed in a stiffening silicone layer (47) impenetrable to light, whereas the lower surface of the frame (43) and the silicone layer (47) are flush with one another, and optical fibres (48) protrude by length L = 0-1000 cm below the flush surfaces and rest directly of a light source (45) situated in a housing (45') forming together a screen (45"); moreover, above the silicone layer (47) between rows of optical fibres (48) arranged alternately are conduits (50) with straight-through holes (50') provided along the whole of their lengths to facilitate distribution of the light-curing material, while ends of the conduits are connected to conduits (51 and 51') supplying and returning the light-curing material to reservoir (52) of a controlling-pumping unit (5).
claims:
Claims Patent claims 1. A printer for 3D printing, comprising a cuboidal cabinet housing with glazed front doors inside which there is a storage container to which, by means of a pump, from a separate container, introduced is systematically a light-curing material up to a preset level and further, comprising a working platform situated above the storage container, said platform being capable to change its position along vertical axis by means of two linear screw drives, whereas all these technical means are coupled with a controller running appropriate software activating said technical means and controlling the process of production of three-dimensional products characterised in that the storage container unit (4) comprises a rectangular frame (43) with peripheral offsets (44), the interior of which is formed by single-mode optical fibres (48) arranged symmetrically to each other and each having a light-transporting core (48') and a coating (48") impenetrable to light, whereas upper ends of the optical fibres are permanently joined with lenses (49) under which situated is a stabilising mesh (55), while lower ends of the optical fibres (48) are straight-through fixed in a stiffening silicone layer (47) impenetrable to light, with lower surface of the frame (43) and the silicone layer (47) being flush with one another and optical fibres (48) protruding by L = 0-1000 cm below the flush surfaces and resting directly on the light source (45) disposed in a casing (45') forming jointly a screen (45"), and moreover, above the silicone layer (47) between rows of optical fibres (48), arranged alternately are conduits (50) with straight-through holes (50') provided along the whole of their lengths to facilitate distribution of the light-curing material, while ends of these conduits are connected to conduits (51 and 5G) supplying and returning, respectively, the light-curing material from and to the reservoir (52) of the controlling-pumping unit (5). 2. The printer according to claim 1 characterised in that below the screen (45") comprising the source of light (45) there is a rectangular frame-shaped insulating element (57) adhering to said source and situated on a frame-shaped rectangular base (58) provided with mounting seats (60) with cylindrical pins (61) mounted in said 

 seats and vibration coils (62) fixed on said seats, whereas ends of these coils are situated in grooves (63) provided in the frame and are connected with electric conductors (64) of a controller (65) controlling operation of the printer. 3. The printer according to claim 1 characterised in that the lenses (49) are converging or diverging lenses. 4. The printer according to claim 1 or 3 characterised in that diameters of the lenses (49) equal from 1 to 1.2 of the diameter of the optical fibres (48). 5. The printer according to claim 1 or 2 characterised in that the source of light (45) is a liquid crystal display (LCD) or light emitting diodes (LEDs) or a projector or ultraviolet (UV) lamps or laser diodes or a laser or sources of optical radiation or radioactive sources. 6. The printer according to claim 1 characterised in that the light-curing material is a resin or a composite material. 7. The printer according to claim 1 characterised in that the diameter of optic fibres (48) is from 1 pm to 1 cm.
